description: SD two-term exactness theorem and spectral action analysis on S³ Dirac spectrum
track: WH1 spectral triple formalization, Step #2 continued

a2_extraction_numerical.method: Extract [K(t) - a0*t^{-3/2} - a1*t^{-1/2}] / t^{1/2} at small t
a2_extraction_numerical.conclusion: Residuals do NOT scale as t^2, stay at floating-point noise ~1e-96 at 100 dps

jacobi_theta_proof.mechanism: K(t) rewrites as theta_2 sum over half-integer eigenvalues; Jacobi inversion gives exactly 2 polynomial terms (a0*t^{-3/2} + a1*t^{-1/2}) plus exponentially small theta_4 remainder

cardano_closed_form.structural_note: Lambda depends on ALL THREE Paper 2 invariants (B, F, Delta) through A

sd_ratio_check.interpretation: rank of spinor bundle on S^3 (Weyl spinors are 2-component)
sd_ratio_check.note: Supertrace F1 claimed ratio=4=dim(spinor); discrepancy is 2×Weyl vs Dirac counting

structural_conclusions: The SD expansion on unit S^3 Dirac terminates at EXACTLY 2 terms (a0, a1)
All higher SD coefficients a_k = 0 for k >= 2, by Jacobi theta modular identity
The CC spectral action equation is a depressed cubic with Cardano closed form
Lambda = 3.710... is determined by INVERTING the spectral action at K/pi, not selected autonomously
The sharp cutoff at Lambda includes exactly 40 = Delta^{-1} modes (Paper 2 n_max=3 boundary)
The CC framework provides an exact functional form but NOT a mechanism to fix Lambda
